name: HubSpot
one_liner: HubSpot is a cloud CRM and inbound marketing/sales/service platform covering contacts, deals, tickets, marketing campaigns, and CMS content.
primary_auth: OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow (required for multi-account/public apps; private-app access tokens for single-account integrations)
access_notes: A free HubSpot CRM account can create private apps with working access tokens at no cost, and a free developer account lets you create OAuth public apps plus up to 10 free 90-day developer test accounts. No payment or human approval is needed for credentials; app review only applies to Marketplace listing.
api_notes: REST API at api.hubapi.com spanning CRM objects (contacts, companies, deals, tickets, custom objects, engagements, associations, properties, pipelines, lists), marketing, CMS/HubDB, automation/workflows, conversations, files, and settings - well over 100 endpoints. Moved to date-based versioning (e.g. /crm/objects/2026-03/contacts) in 2026; a GraphQL API is also offered for querying HubSpot data. Legacy portal API keys were deprecated Nov 2022 in favor of private app tokens.
mcp_notes: HubSpot ships an official hosted remote MCP server (mcp.hubspot.com, Streamable HTTP, OAuth 2.1 with PKCE) giving read/write access to CRM records and activities, plus a local CLI-based developer-platform MCP server. Documented at developers.hubspot.com/mcp; announced in public beta then rolled out broadly. Many community servers also exist.

evidence.claim: HubSpot's public API supports two auth methods - OAuth for public/multi-account apps (required for Marketplace listings) and private app access tokens for single-account integrations; legacy API keys are deprecated
API reference shows REST endpoints at api.hubapi.com with new date-based versioning (e.g. GET /crm/objects/2026-03/contacts)
Free developer accounts and free developer test accounts (up to 10, 90-day renewable) provide self-serve API access; private apps and APIs work on free HubSpot accounts
Webhooks API lets apps subscribe to CRM events such as contact.creation and propertyChange, managed via /webhooks/2026-3/{appId}/subscriptions
HubSpot publishes an official MCP server page covering its remote CRM MCP server and developer MCP server

open_questions: Exact current GA-vs-beta status of the remote MCP server was not verified from a fetched changelog page (search results indicated public beta in 2025 then GA in April 2026). Account-types details (10 test accounts, 90-day renewal) came from official docs surfaced in search summaries rather than a direct page fetch.
